name: Ruck It Up
one_liner: Ruck 3x per week with 20-40lb for a month
evidence_summary: Loaded carries improve bone density, metabolic conditioning, and grip strength
board_quote: Rucking is Zone 2 with a strength stimulus. Two adaptations for the price of one.
protocol: 3 walks per week with 20-40lb ruck. Minimum 30 minutes each.

name: 10K Steps
one_liner: Hit 10,000 steps every single day for 14 days
evidence_summary: 10,000 steps/day associated with 40-50% reduction in all-cause mortality
board_quote: The simplest longevity intervention that exists.
protocol: 10,000 steps minimum per day. Auto-verified from Garmin/Apple Health.

name: Morning Light Walk
one_liner: Outdoor walk within 30 min of waking for 21 days
evidence_summary: Morning sunlight resets circadian rhythm, improves cortisol awakening response
board_quote: Morning sunlight within 30 minutes of waking is the single highest-ROI protocol.
protocol: Walk outdoors within 30 min of waking. Minimum 10 min. No sunglasses.

name: Run a 5K
one_liner: Complete a 5K run without stopping
evidence_summary: VO2max is the strongest predictor of all-cause mortality
board_quote: Building cardiovascular capacity is non-negotiable for the centenarian decathlon.
protocol: Complete a 5K (3.1 miles) running continuously.

name: 60-Second Dead Hang
one_liner: Hang from a bar for 60 seconds without dropping
evidence_summary: Grip strength is one of the strongest predictors of all-cause mortality
board_quote: Centenarian decathlon benchmark.
protocol: Hang from a pull-up bar with both hands for 60 continuous seconds.

name: Always Take the Stairs
one_liner: No elevators or escalators for 14 days
evidence_summary: Stair climbing associated with reduced cardiovascular mortality
board_quote: The easiest way to add NEAT to your day.
protocol: Take stairs every time. Zero elevator or escalator use.

name: 20 Consecutive Pushups
one_liner: Complete 20 full pushups without stopping
evidence_summary: Pushup capacity inversely associated with cardiovascular events
board_quote: Simple, measurable, functional strength benchmark.
protocol: 20 full pushups, chest to ground, no pausing.

name: Screens Off
one_liner: No screens 1 hour before bed for 14 days
evidence_summary: Blue light suppresses melatonin by up to 50%
board_quote: The single most impactful sleep hygiene change most people can make.
protocol: All screens off 60 minutes before target bedtime.

name: Clockwork Sleep
one_liner: Same bedtime ±30 min for 14 straight days
evidence_summary: Irregular sleep timing increases cardiovascular risk
board_quote: Social jetlag is the silent killer of consistency.
protocol: In bed within ±30 min of target bedtime every night. Auto-verified from Whoop.

name: Bedroom Detox
one_liner: Sleep with phone in another room for 7 days
evidence_summary: Phone proximity disrupts sleep onset
board_quote: The phone is a dopamine slot machine. It does not belong where you sleep.
protocol: Phone charges in a different room. Use an alarm clock.

name: Protein Perfect
one_liner: Hit 190g protein target every day for 14 days
evidence_summary: High protein preserves lean mass during deficit
board_quote: Adherence is the best diet. But protein adherence is non-negotiable in a deficit.
protocol: 190g protein minimum per day via MacroFactor.

name: No DoorDash
one_liner: Zero food delivery apps for 30 days
evidence_summary: Food delivery associated with higher caloric intake
board_quote: DoorDash is the avoidance pattern made frictionless.
protocol: No DoorDash, UberEats, Grubhub. Cook or eat out in person.

name: Green With Every Meal
one_liner: Eat a vegetable with every meal for 14 days
evidence_summary: Higher vegetable intake reduces all-cause mortality
board_quote: Micronutrient density starts at the plate.
protocol: Every meal includes at least one serving of vegetables.

name: Total Transparency
one_liner: Log every meal in MacroFactor for 30 straight days
evidence_summary: Self-monitoring doubles weight loss outcomes
board_quote: You can't manage what you don't measure. Logging IS the intervention.
protocol: Every meal logged at time of eating. No 'I'll log it later.'

name: Home Chef
one_liner: Cook at home 5+ meals per week for 30 days
evidence_summary: Home cooking associated with better diet quality
board_quote: Cooking isn't about perfection — it's about control.
protocol: Cook at least 5 meals at home per week.

name: Morning Pages
one_liner: Journal every morning for 14 days before phone
evidence_summary: Expressive writing reduces anxiety (Pennebaker, multiple RCTs)
board_quote: The journal is where you meet yourself before the world tells you who to be today.
protocol: Write for at least 5 min every morning before any app or email.

name: Gratitude Practice
one_liner: Write 3 things you're grateful for every night for 21 days
evidence_summary: Gratitude journaling improves wellbeing and sleep quality
board_quote: Gratitude isn't toxic positivity. It's teaching the brain to notice what's already working.
protocol: Every night, write 3 specific things. Not 'family' but 'the way Brittany laughed at dinner.'

name: Daily NSDR
one_liner: 10 minutes of NSDR/meditation daily for 14 days
evidence_summary: NSDR improves dopamine by 65%, reduces stress
board_quote: NSDR is the highest-ROI stress tool. Zero cost, 10 minutes.
protocol: 10 min of NSDR or body scan meditation daily.

name: Slow Looking
one_liner: Visit a museum and sit with one painting for 30+ min
evidence_summary: Sustained attention to art improves focus, reduces rumination
board_quote: Truly seeing something without judgment is meditation the anxious mind desperately needs.
protocol: Go to an art museum. Pick one painting. Sit with it 30+ min. No phone.

name: Unsent Letter
one_liner: Write a letter of appreciation to someone
evidence_summary: Expressing gratitude increases wellbeing for writer and recipient
board_quote: Vulnerability is the price of admission to meaningful connection.
protocol: Write a letter to someone who impacted your life. 200+ words. Sending optional.

name: Digital Detox
one_liner: No social media for 7 days
evidence_summary: Social media breaks reduce anxiety and depression
board_quote: A 7-day reset recalibrates your reward circuitry.
protocol: Delete social media apps. No IG, X, Reddit, TikTok. Messaging fine.

name: Read, Don't Scroll
one_liner: Read 30 min before bed instead of phone for 14 days
evidence_summary: Reading before bed improves sleep onset latency
board_quote: A physical book is one of the few wind-down activities that genuinely works.
protocol: 30 min physical book reading before bed. No backlit e-reader.

name: Weekly Coffee Date
one_liner: Coffee with a different friend weekly for a month
evidence_summary: Regular social contact reduces mortality risk by 50%
board_quote: Social connection is not a luxury. It is the foundation.
protocol: One in-person coffee/lunch/walk with a friend per week. Different person if possible.

name: Talk to a Stranger
one_liner: Brief conversation with a stranger every day for 7 days
evidence_summary: Brief social interactions boost mood, reduce social anxiety
board_quote: Every conversation with a stranger is exposure therapy.
protocol: Initiate a brief exchange. Compliment, question, comment. Min 30 seconds.

name: Pick Up the Phone
one_liner: Call (not text) one person per day for 7 days
evidence_summary: Voice communication creates stronger social bonds than text
board_quote: A text is a transaction. A phone call is a connection.
protocol: Call one person per day. Friend, family, or colleague. Must be voice.

name: Yes Week
one_liner: Say yes to every social invitation for 14 days
evidence_summary: Exposure therapy for social anxiety through repeated engagement
board_quote: Your default is 'no.' Flip the default and watch what happens.
protocol: Say yes to every social invitation. Only exception: genuine conflict.

name: Host a Dinner
one_liner: Plan and host a dinner for 4+ people
evidence_summary: Communal meals strengthen social bonds
board_quote: Hosting creates belonging. It doesn't have to be fancy — it has to be real.
protocol: Host dinner at home for 4+ people. Cook or order — the gathering is the point.

name: Daily Compliment
one_liner: Genuine compliment to a stranger every day for 7 days
evidence_summary: Prosocial behavior increases positive affect
board_quote: A compliment costs nothing and creates genuine human connection.
protocol: One genuine face-to-face compliment to a stranger daily. Be specific.

name: Join Something
one_liner: Attend a group class/club/meetup weekly for a month
evidence_summary: Group membership reduces mortality risk comparable to quitting smoking
board_quote: Your social muscle atrophies without group engagement.
protocol: One group activity per week. Class, club, meetup, sports league. Not the gym.

name: T0 Perfect Fortnight
one_liner: Complete all Tier 0 habits for 14 straight days
evidence_summary: Keystone habit consistency creates identity reinforcement
board_quote: 14 days of T0 is proof you can close the knowing-doing gap.
protocol: Every T0 habit every day for 14 consecutive days. One miss resets.

name: No Snooze
one_liner: No snooze button for 7 straight days
evidence_summary: Snoozing fragments sleep architecture
board_quote: The first decision of the day sets the tone.
protocol: Alarm goes off, feet on floor. Zero snooze presses.

name: Cold Finish
one_liner: End every shower with 30 sec cold for 14 days
evidence_summary: Cold exposure increases norepinephrine 200-300%
board_quote: Learning to lean into chosen discomfort transfers to every other domain.
protocol: End of every shower: coldest setting, 30 seconds minimum.

name: Make the Bed
one_liner: Make your bed every morning for 21 days
evidence_summary: Keystone habit research: small control acts cascade
board_quote: You can't control the world but you can control your bed.
protocol: Make bed within 10 min of getting up. Every morning.

name: 5-Minute Reset
one_liner: 5-minute tidy-up every evening for 14 days
evidence_summary: Environmental order reduces cognitive load
board_quote: Five minutes of tidying is five minutes of reclaiming control.
protocol: 5-minute timer every evening. Tidy living space. Stop when timer ends.

name: Lights Out by 10
one_liner: In bed with lights off by 10pm every night for two weeks
evidence_summary: Consistent early bedtime aligns with circadian cortisol drop and improves sleep onset latency
board_quote: The most powerful sleep drug is a consistent bedtime. Nothing else comes close.
protocol: Lights off by 10:00pm PDT every night. Track with Whoop bedtime metric. 1 grace night allowed.

name: Noon Caffeine Cutoff
one_liner: No caffeine after 12pm for 21 days
evidence_summary: Caffeine half-life is 5-6 hours; afternoon intake elevates adenosine blocking at bedtime
board_quote: Half-life means half is still circulating. At 6pm you're still 50% caffeinated from a 12pm coffee.
protocol: No coffee, tea, pre-workout, or caffeine supplements after 12:00pm. Decaf allowed. Track compliance daily.

name: Cave Mode
one_liner: Optimize bedroom: blackout, 65°F, no devices for 14 days
evidence_summary: Cool (65-68°F), dark, quiet sleep environments improve deep sleep % by 15-25%
board_quote: Your bedroom should be a cave. Cool, dark, boring. That's the entire protocol.
protocol: Blackout curtains or eye mask, thermostat to 65°F or Eight Sleep cooling, phone charges outside bedroom. Measure deep sleep % change.

name: The Ritual
one_liner: Same 30-minute wind-down routine every night for 21 days
evidence_summary: Consistent pre-sleep routines signal circadian system and reduce sleep onset latency
board_quote: Routine is a signal. Your body learns 'these actions mean sleep is coming.'
protocol: Design a 30-min sequence (e.g. stretch, journal, read). Same order, same time, every night. Log compliance.

name: First Light
one_liner: Get outside within 30 min of waking for 14 days
evidence_summary: Morning light exposure anchors circadian clock, improves cortisol awakening response
board_quote: Huberman's #1: morning sunlight within 30 minutes sets your entire circadian day.
protocol: Step outside within 30 minutes of waking. 10 min minimum, no sunglasses. Overcast days still count. Track daily.

name: Heat Therapy
one_liner: Sauna 3x/week for 20 min at 170°F+ for a month
evidence_summary: Laukkanen et al.: 4-7 sauna sessions/week associated with 40% reduction in all-cause mortality
board_quote: The Finnish data is staggering. Regular sauna use is one of the strongest longevity signals we have.
protocol: 3 sessions/week, 170-190°F, 15-20 min. Cool down 5 min between rounds if doing multiple. Hydrate aggressively. Log sessions.

name: Cold Steel
one_liner: Cold shower finish (2 min) or cold plunge daily for 21 days
evidence_summary: Cold exposure increases norepinephrine 200-300%, improves mood, reduces inflammation markers
board_quote: The discomfort IS the mechanism. Deliberate cold trains your stress response system.
protocol: End every shower with 2+ minutes of cold (as cold as it goes), OR cold plunge 50-59°F for 2 min. Morning preferred. Log daily.

name: Couch to 5K
one_liner: Complete a Couch to 5K program (8 weeks)
evidence_summary: Progressive running programs build aerobic base safely; VO2max improvement 15-20%
board_quote: The evidence for running and all-cause mortality is bulletproof. Getting to a 5K is the single highest ROI fitness goal.
protocol: Follow structured C25K plan (3 runs/week, walk-run intervals progressing to continuous). Track with Strava. Target: run 5K without stopping.

name: Zone 2 Machine
one_liner: Hit 150 min/week of Zone 2 cardio for a month
evidence_summary: WHO + Attia: 150 min/week moderate cardio is the longevity minimum. Zone 2 specifically improves mitochondrial function.
board_quote: Zone 2 is boring. It's also the single most important exercise modality for longevity.
protocol: 150+ minutes per week in HR Zone 2 (roughly 60-70% max HR). Can be walking, cycling, rowing, swimming. Track via Whoop/Strava.

name: Limber Up
one_liner: 15 min mobility/stretching every day for 21 days
evidence_summary: Daily mobility work reduces injury risk, improves recovery, maintains joint range of motion
board_quote: Flexibility is the pillar people skip. It catches up with you after 40.
protocol: 15 minutes daily: hip openers, thoracic spine, hamstrings, shoulders. Can be yoga, foam rolling, or structured routine. Track compliance.

name: Post-Meal Walk
one_liner: 10-minute walk within 30 min of every meal for 14 days
evidence_summary: Post-meal walking reduces glucose spike by 30-50% (CGM data); improves insulin sensitivity
board_quote: The cheapest glucose drug in the world is a 10-minute walk after dinner.
protocol: Walk 10+ minutes within 30 min of each main meal. All 3 meals ideal, minimum 1/day (dinner). Track with steps + CGM correlation.

name: Grip of Steel
one_liner: Dead hangs + farmer carries 3x/week for a month
evidence_summary: Grip strength is the single strongest predictor of all-cause mortality in exercise science
board_quote: If you can only measure one physical attribute for longevity prediction, measure grip strength.
protocol: 3x/week: 3 sets max dead hang + 2 sets farmer carry (heavy, 30 sec). Track hang time progression.

name: Sugar Free
one_liner: No added sugar for 14 days
evidence_summary: Added sugar drives insulin resistance, inflammation, and triglyceride elevation
board_quote: Two weeks without added sugar resets your palate and your insulin sensitivity.
protocol: No foods with added sugar in ingredients. Whole fruit OK. Read every label. Track compliance + CGM response.

name: Time-Restricted Eating
one_liner: 16:8 intermittent fasting for 21 days
evidence_summary: 16:8 TRE improves insulin sensitivity, may reduce inflammation markers; mixed evidence on weight loss vs caloric restriction alone
board_quote: The evidence is nuanced. TRE probably helps via calorie control and circadian alignment, not magic.
protocol: Eating window 12pm-8pm (or similar 8-hour window). Water, black coffee, tea allowed outside. Track window compliance.

name: Prep Day
one_liner: Meal prep every Sunday for a month
evidence_summary: Meal prepping correlates with higher diet quality, lower impulse eating, and reduced food delivery reliance
board_quote: DoorDash is the enemy. Sunday prep is the fortress.
protocol: Every Sunday: prep 4+ meals for the week. Protein + veg + complex carb. Photograph and log. Track DoorDash orders (target: zero).

name: Water Works
one_liner: Drink 100oz of water daily for 14 days
evidence_summary: Adequate hydration improves cognitive function, exercise performance, and satiety signals
board_quote: Most people are chronically mildly dehydrated. It affects everything from focus to hunger.
protocol: 100oz (3L) water daily. Coffee/tea don't count. Use marked water bottle. Log intake.

name: Veggies First
one_liner: Eat vegetables before carbs at every meal for 14 days
evidence_summary: Eating fiber before carbohydrates reduces glucose spike by 30-40% (Jesse Inchauspé / CGM studies)
board_quote: Same meal, different order, dramatically different glucose response. The data is clear.
protocol: At every meal with carbs, eat the vegetables and protein first, carbs last. Track with CGM.

name: Box Breathing
one_liner: 5 min box breathing daily for 21 days
evidence_summary: Cyclic sighing / box breathing reduces cortisol and improves HRV within a single session (Huberman Lab)
board_quote: Five minutes of structured breathing does more for acute stress than any supplement.
protocol: 5 min daily: 4-count inhale, 4-count hold, 4-count exhale, 4-count hold. Morning or before bed. Track HRV response.

name: Structured Journal
one_liner: Write in a structured CBT-style journal daily for 21 days
evidence_summary: Expressive writing and structured journaling reduce anxiety and depressive symptoms (Pennebaker, multiple RCTs)
board_quote: Journaling isn't therapy. But it uses the same mechanism: externalizing thought patterns makes them examinable.
protocol: Daily 10-min journal using format: Situation → Thought → Emotion → Evidence For/Against → Reframe. Track mood before/after.

name: 10 Minutes Still
one_liner: 10 min meditation daily for 30 days
evidence_summary: 8-week meditation programs show measurable changes in amygdala reactivity and prefrontal cortex thickness
board_quote: The evidence base for meditation is now enormous. 10 minutes daily is the minimum effective dose.
protocol: 10 min daily: guided (Waking Up, Headspace) or unguided. Same time each day preferred. Track streak and subjective calm rating.

name: Forest Bath
one_liner: 30 min in nature (no phone) 3x/week for a month
evidence_summary: Shinrin-yoku research: 30 min in nature reduces cortisol 12-16%, lowers blood pressure, improves NK cell activity
board_quote: The Japanese have known this for decades. Nature exposure is a legitimate clinical intervention.
protocol: 3x/week: 30+ min outdoors in green space. Phone on airplane mode. Walk, sit, or hike. Log location and subjective mood shift.

name: Worry Window
one_liner: Contain all worrying to a 15-min daily window for 14 days
evidence_summary: Stimulus control for worry (scheduled worry time) reduces generalized anxiety symptoms (CBT literature)
board_quote: Paradoxically, giving yourself permission to worry at a set time reduces total worry time.
protocol: Pick a 15-min window each day (not before bed). Write all worries during that window. Outside the window, defer: 'I'll worry about that at 5pm.' Track worry frequency outside window.

name: Present Meals
one_liner: No phones at the dinner table for 14 days
evidence_summary: Phone presence reduces conversation depth and empathy even when not in use (Przybylski & Weinstein, 2013)
board_quote: The phone on the table is a third person in the conversation. Remove it.
protocol: Phone stays in another room during dinner. Both partners/all household members commit. Track compliance and conversation quality.

name: Reach Back
one_liner: Contact one old friend you've lost touch with each week for a month
evidence_summary: Weak ties reactivation improves wellbeing and social network resilience (Granovetter + Murthy)
board_quote: The friends you've drifted from aren't gone. One text reopens the door.
protocol: One text/call per week to someone you haven't spoken to in 3+ months. No agenda, just reconnecting. Log the interaction.

name: Open Up
one_liner: Share something vulnerable with someone you trust once a week for a month
evidence_summary: Vulnerability in relationships deepens trust and connection (Brené Brown research; Murthy loneliness framework)
board_quote: Depth of connection matters more than frequency. One vulnerable conversation outweighs ten surface ones.
protocol: Once per week: share something real (a fear, a struggle, a hope) with someone you trust. Can be in person, phone, or letter. Journal about it after.

name: Find Your Tribe
one_liner: Attend the same group/class/meetup weekly for a month
evidence_summary: Regular group participation is the strongest predictor of social wellbeing (Murthy, Holt-Lunstad meta-analysis)
board_quote: Belonging requires showing up. Not once. Repeatedly. Same place, same people.
protocol: Pick one: running club, gym class, book club, volunteer group, church, coding meetup. Attend weekly, same time. Track attendance.

name: Daily Thanks
one_liner: Send one genuine gratitude text per day for 14 days
evidence_summary: Expressing gratitude improves both sender and receiver wellbeing; strengthens social bonds (Emmons & McCullough)
board_quote: This is the highest ROI social habit. 30 seconds, one text, two people feel better.
protocol: One text per day to a different person: specific, genuine thanks for something they did or are. Not generic. Track who and what.

name: Clear Mind
one_liner: No cannabis for 30 days
evidence_summary: Cannabis disrupts REM sleep architecture; 30-day abstinence restores sleep stages and improves motivation
board_quote: REM is where emotional processing happens. Cannabis suppresses it. The dreams come back — that's the healing.
protocol: Zero cannabis in any form for 30 days. Track sleep quality, dream recall, motivation, and cravings daily. Use vice streak tracker.

name: Eyes Forward
one_liner: No pornography for 30 days
evidence_summary: Pornography abstinence associated with improved motivation, reduced compulsive behavior, and restored dopamine sensitivity
board_quote: The dopamine circuit doesn't care about the source. Superstimulus withdrawal restores baseline.
protocol: Zero pornography in any form for 30 days. Note triggers, use redirect strategies. Track via vice streak. Journal urges.

name: Clockwork
one_liner: Wake at the exact same time (±15 min) for 21 days — weekends included
evidence_summary: Consistent wake time is the single strongest circadian anchor; social jet lag disrupts metabolic health
board_quote: Your body doesn't know it's Saturday. Social jet lag is real jet lag.
protocol: Set one wake time. Hit it within 15 min every day including weekends. No snooze. Track with Whoop wake time.

name: Dopamine Reset
one_liner: No social media, video games, or streaming for 7 days
evidence_summary: Temporary abstinence from high-dopamine stimuli restores baseline sensitivity and improves capacity for delayed gratification
board_quote: Seven days without superstimuli makes everything else feel interesting again. Books, walks, conversations.
protocol: Delete social media apps, no YouTube/TikTok/Netflix/gaming for 7 days. News OK via text only. Replace with books, walks, conversation. Journal daily.
